summary: Tested public prefill endpoint (privacy-first, no-enumeration) and BookingPage step-4 UX prefill. Backend pytest 4/4 PASSED. Frontend 7/7 e2e checks PASSED after manual frontend supervisor restart (webpack hadn't picked up new BookingPage code until restart).

minor.issue: Rate limit 20/minute did not trigger after 25 sequential public requests from the same client. The route uses a *local* Limiter() instance in routes/public.py (line 21), and main.py does NOT add SlowAPIMiddleware to the FastAPI app. The decorator-based limit may be a no-op behind kubernetes ingress (all requests share proxy IP, but the limiter never blocks). Privacy guarantees remain intact (always 200, no enumeration), but the documented brute-force mitigation is not enforced.

action_items: (Minor) Wire the prefill rate-limiter to the actual app: either reuse `app.state.limiter` from main.py instead of creating a fresh `Limiter()` in routes/public.py, and/or add `SlowAPIMiddleware` to the FastAPI app and a `RateLimitExceeded` handler. Without this, /api/public/prefill is effectively unrestricted.

(Note) Frontend hot-reload did not compile the newly-added BookingPage prefill code automatically; a `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` was required for `data-testid="booking-prefill-hint"` and `tryPrefillFromEmail` to appear in the bundle. After restart, all behaviours work correctly. No action needed unless hot-reload pattern repeats.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/public.py creates its own `Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address)` local to the module instead of importing the app-level limiter; combined with the missing SlowAPIMiddleware in main.py, the @limiter.limit decorators in this file are not enforced. Confirmed empirically (25 consecutive requests, all 200).

Privacy contract on /api/public/prefill is exemplary: response is whitelisted to {school_name, contact_name, contact_phone, group_type, age_or_class, num_students, num_teachers, special_requirements}; no IDs, dates, or program/institution refs are leaked, and unknown e-mails still return 200 + {found:false}.
BookingPage.js tryPrefillFromEmail dedupes via prefilledFromEmail state — verified one API call per unique e-mail blur, regardless of repeated blur events.
Undo flow restores the pre-prefill snapshot correctly; only-fill-empty policy holds (manually-typed school_name='Moje škola' is preserved across prefill).
BookingPage.js is now 1083 lines — getting close to the 'split into smaller modules' threshold; consider extracting the contact form + prefill hook in a future iteration.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (hint, prefill, toast, undo, dedupe, only-fill-empty, unknown-email all OK)

test_credentials: Public booking page — no auth. Test institution: eefb9cbf-52bf-4e20-9418-5b2f659f8d23 (Galerie Bude živo). Seed reservation present for jana.novakova@zs-botanicka.cz.
seed_data_creation: Did not create seed; pre-existing jana.novakova@zs-botanicka.cz reservation was found in DB and exercised through both backend and frontend tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public /api/public/prefill is correct on privacy/data-shape. Only outstanding concern is that the decorator-based rate limit isn't actually enforced (no SlowAPIMiddleware, local Limiter instance in routes/public.py). Frontend BookingPage step-4 prefill UX is fully working. If main agent edits BookingPage.js again, remember webpack hot-reload may need a manual frontend restart for new test selectors to land in the bundle.
rca of the issue: Initial frontend run reported PREFILL HINT MISSING and 0 calls to /api/public/prefill — root cause: webpack-dev-server bundle.js did not contain the newly added tryPrefillFromEmail / booking-prefill-hint code (verified by curl-grep on bundle.js → 0 matches). After `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` and ~35s recompile, the bundle contained both symbols and all e2e behaviours passed. Mitigation: when freshly-added React code isn't visible in DOM, grep the served bundle and restart frontend if missing.
